Functions of Deans of Schools of Studies £ Duties of Heads of Department 1 lim n and Conditions of Appointment of Honorary Professors and Emeritus Professors 4 Appointment of Examiners and Moderators In terms of Sectkm 43 of MsulartsAxad National Urdu University Act, 1996 (No: 2 of 1997) these Ordinances are to be published in the Official Gazette and also to be laid before) each house of the Parliament. FUNCTIONS OF DEANS OF SCHOOLS OF STUDIES (AetS127(lK«dSWito.7(3)} , % In exercise of dw powers .vested in Section 27(1X«D A Statute 7(3) qf the University Act, dw Executive Council, on the (hrdfoaqqe proposed by foe Academic Council, hereby makes foe following Ordinance regarding Deans of Schools of foe University. 1. The Dean of a School shall be the Head of the School ' and shall be responsible for the conduct and maintenance of standards of teaching and research in the School. He shall be the Chairman and Convener of the Board of foe School. £ The Ddah shall have the following powers and (tj To co-ordinate and generally supervise the teaching and research work in the School forough the Head of Departments/Centres; (b) To take steps to promote inter-disciplinary teaching and research, wherever necessary; (c) lb keep a record of the evaluation .of sessional work and of foe attendance of the students at lectures, tutorials, seminars or practical* wherever ■' *'. these are prescribed; (d) lb arrange for the examination of the University in respect of the students of the School in accordance with such directions as may be given by the Board of Schools of studies of the Academic Council (e) To take steps to give affect to the decision and recommendations of foe School; and IS)lc In ex(mtiSe # & ^ ^ ^ j ^ j|'S ectk» 27(lXq) A Statute 8(5) of fob U fo v « y rx ^ t^lrecu tiv e Council, on foe Ordinance proposed^ m Council, hereby makes the following Ordinance regrading Heads df Depa ment of,fog University. ' The Head of a Department/Centre convene and preside over meetings of die Department/Centre, and shall foe gofers! supervision of the Dean:— a. organize foe teaching ard research woric in the b. fiame foe time table in confonnity witii the allocation of foe teaching work made by foe Department/Centre; c. maintain discipline in foe class rooms and laboratories through teachers; d. assign to the teachers in tire Department/Centre such duties as msy be necessary for the proper fcnctionmg of (foe Department or. Centre; and assign work to and exercise control over tbe non-tcachifigstafF in die Department/Centre; snd e. perform such other functions as may be assigned to him by the Dean, the Board of the School concerned, the Academic Council, the Executive Council and tire Vice Chancellor. TERMS AND CONDmONS OF APPOINTMENT OF HONORARY PROFESSORS AND EMERTTUS PROFESSORS [A d S. 5(xyX 27(lX<j)J In exercise o f dw powers vested in Section 27(lX q) of the University A d, the Executive Council, on the Ordinance proposed by foe Academic Council, heteby makes foe following Ordinance regarding terms and conditions o f appointment o f honorary Professors and Emeritus 1 o f foe University. ‘ HONORARY PROFESSOR 1. A distinguished scholar who is either in ictive service or on .superannuation may be considered. for appointment as Honorary Professor by foe Executive Council on foe recommendation of the Dean of the School or Vice-Chancellor ' £ The duration of appointment shall be 3 years. 3. Honorary Professors will not be paid any salary cr remuneration. However, he/she will be provided local hospitality and travel expenses from the dace of his/ . her residence to the University and bade whenever he/she visits the University for delivering lectures and for participating in any other academic activity of the School. 3. 4 1 i3(; the mal exa Ni m PC Pn Co Pa Cc Cc M ■ r Ph -- 4 of 6 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA, DECEMBER 2,2006;(AGRAHAYANA II, 1928) 11315 Fart 111 — S ec. 4] EMERITUS PROFESSOR 1. A Professor who has retired from the University may be invited by the Executive Council to continue his/ hec research/teaching activities in the University as an Emeritus Professor after his/her superannuation. 2 J h e duration of appointment shall be 3 years yvith a - provision for renewal. . - . 3, The Emeritus Professor will not be paid any salary or remuneration. 4. He/she will be provided office space and other facilities to can * out his/her research/teaching activities during / his/her tenure. A ppo in t m e n t of ex a m in ers a n d m oderators [Act S. 27(g) Statute 13(2)(xHi)T In exesrcise of the powers vested in Section 27(g) Statute 13(2)(xiii) of the University Act, the Executive Council, on the Ordinance proposed by the Academic Council, hereby makes the following Ordinance regarding appointment of examines and moderators of the University. 1. The list of the examiners and Moderators for. all examinations, except for Ph.D. degree shall be drawn up by tiw Board of Studies and submitted to die School of Studies concerned. The School of Studies shall scrutinize the list and forward the same to the Executive Council for approval in consultation with die Academic Council, 2. The Examiners for Ph D. Degree shall be appointed by die Executive Council in consultation with Academic Council on die recommendation of die School Board j, ofthe School concerned. „ . I 3. The question papers for examination .in each subject of studies offered by die University shall be moderated by a committee consisting o f: a. Head of die Depaiinent concerned; and b. Not less than one and not more than two other persons appointed by die Vice Chancellor for (he purpose. Provided that one of the Moderators may be an Exapiiner in the concerned examination not engaged in teaching in the University. , i 4. The question papers shall be moderated in accordance with the regulations; 5. a. If a Board of Examiner* sets a question paper and evaluate* the answer books, each external exam iner shall be paid full remuneration specified in Columns (II), (III) above. c . When sn infwof book l§ cxuniscd by twoextemal examinerjointly, each examiner shall be paid die fell remuneration as specified in Cotamn (IB) above. d . hi respect of a subject in which a question paper is set in two parts separately by two external j examiners and the answer bodes thereof are also evaluated by them independently of m :h other, the remuneration payable to each examiner for setting the question paper and for evaluation of answer books shall be three-fifth of die rates specified in Column II, III for the paper and evaluation of answer books. e . If an external examiner examines answer books in respect of the question paper not set by him, he shall be paid for examining answer books at die rates a4 specified in Column (II) subject to a minimum fee equivalent to half the fee specified' therein for setting a question paper for the £ If more than half of a question paper is c hange the Committee of Moderators, the Vice Chana may on the recommendation of tire Convene the Committee, direct that no remuneration be { for setting tire question paper, g. Iftwo or more external examiners conduct the V Voce, die remuneration shaO be divided equ among them. The remuneration shall not be paid to extei examiners until the Registrar has received the Aw m ] Q Sheets, the Answer Books, the Report of Examiners and such other statements as drey n be required to prepare. L j- If the external examiners do not send in th complete document referred to in (h) above by ■ appointed date, the remuneration payable to th a be reduced by an amount calculated at Rs. per day for the first seven days of delay a Rs. 10/- per day thereafter, unless the Vi Chancellor is satisfied that the delay was due cause beyond the control of Examiners.
